Use this skill before committing security-sensitive code or during review.

## Concepts (timeless)
- Validate and canonicalize all untrusted input at the boundary; reject by default.
- Parameterize queries (no string-built SQL); encode/escape output (no XSS); enforce CSRF on state-changing requests.
- Authn proves identity; authz checks it on every resource access — never trust the client's claim.
- Secrets live in a secret manager/env, never in source, logs, or error messages. Rotate on exposure.
- Least privilege everywhere (tokens, DB roles, service accounts); rate-limit public endpoints.

## Pull current docs first
Threats and platform mitigations change — check current guidance for THIS stack:
- OWASP Top 10: https://owasp.org/www-project-top-ten/
- OWASP ASVS: https://owasp.org/www-project-application-security-verification-standard/
- OWASP Top 10 / ASVS for the current canonical checklist.

## Checklist
- [ ] No hardcoded secrets; secrets sourced from a manager/env
- [ ] Inputs validated; queries parameterized; output encoded
- [ ] Authz enforced on every protected resource (not just authn)
- [ ] CSRF protection on state-changing routes; CORS scoped
- [ ] Error messages + logs leak no secrets/PII; rate limits in place
